title: Ironic Poem About Prostitution
content:
When I was young and had no sense
    In far-off Mandalay
    I lost my heart to a Burmese girl
    As lovely as the day.

    Her skin was gold, her hair was jet,
    Her teeth were ivory;
    I said, �for twenty silver pieces,
    Maiden, sleep with me�.

    She looked at me, so pure, so sad,
    The loveliest thing alive,
    And in her lisping, virgin voice,
    Stood out for twenty-five.
meaning:
George Orwell (Eric Arthur Blair)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Ironic Poem About Prostitution"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
